title: Actuators and methods of use
abstract: Actuators and methods of use are provided. An actuator may comprise an inner member made from an elastic material and defining a compartment for receiving an actuating fluid, the inner member being moveable in a longitudinal direction from a relaxed state to an expanded state by introducing an actuating fluid into the inner member; and an outer member made from an inelastic material and being disposed around the elastic inner member to control expansion of the elastic inner member in a radial direction, the outer member being moveable from a folded relaxed configuration to an unfolded extended configuration as the inner member moves from the relaxed state to the expanded state, wherein the movement of the actuator between the relaxed state and the expanded state causes a movement of a structure to which the actuator is attached.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A hydraulically operated lattice device comprising: a first member; a second member; and an actuator connected to the first member at its first end and to the second member at its second end, the actuator comprising: an inner member made from an elastic material and defining a compartment for receiving an actuating fluid, the inner member being moveable in a longitudinal direction from a relaxed state to an expanded state by introducing an actuating fluid into the inner member; and an outer member made from an inelastic material and being disposed around the elastic inner member to control expansion of the elastic inner member in a radial direction, the outer member being moveable from a folded relaxed configuration to an unfolded extended configuration as the inner member moves from the relaxed state to the expanded state, wherein the movement of the actuator between the relaxed state and the expanded state cause a movement of at least one of the first member and the second member relative to the other member. 2. The lattice device of claiml wherein the outer member is connected to the elastic inner member discontinuously at one or more contact points. 3. The lattice device of any one of claims 1-2 wherein the outer member is connected to the elastic inner member discontinuously at a first end and a second end. 4. The lattice device of any one of claims 1-3 wherein the outer member in a fully extended state forms an uninterrupted outer barrier preventing a radial protrusion of the inner member through the outer member. The lattice device of any one of claims 1-4 wherein the first member and the second member are pivotally connected by a hinge. The lattice device of any one of claims 1-5 wherein the actuator is axially symmetric. The lattice device of any one of claims 1-6 wherein the actuator is axially asymmetric having at least two segments with different elastic properties. The lattice device of any one of claims 1-7 wherein the actuator is pressurized to a pressure sufficient to fully radially expand the actuator such that additional pressurization of the actuator results in expansion in longitudinal direction only. An actuator system comprising: a reservoir including an actuating fluid; and multiple actuators, each of the multiple actuators comprising: an inner member made from an elastic material and defining a compartment for receiving an actuating fluid, the inner member being moveable in a longitudinal direction from a relaxed state to an expanded state by introducing an actuating fluid into the inner member; and an outer member made from an inelastic material and being disposed around the elastic inner member to control expansion of the elastic inner member in a radial direction, the outer member being moveable from a folded relaxed configuration to an unfolded extended configuration as the inner member moves from the relaxed state to the expanded state; a pump in fluid communication with the reservoir and the actuators for selectively supplying the actuating fluid to the actuators; and a controller in communication with the pump to control operation of the pump. 10. The actuator system of claim 9 wherein the outer member is connected to the elastic inner member discontinuously at one or more contact points. 11. The actuator system of any one of claims 9-10 wherein the outer member is connected to the elastic inner member discontinuously at a first end and a second end. 12. The actuator system of any one of claims 9-11 wherein the outer member in a fully extended state forms an uninterrupted outer barrier preventing a radial protrusion of the inner member through the outer member. 13. The actuator system of any one of claims 9-12 wherein the actuator is axially symmetric. 14. The actuator system of any one of claims 9-13 wherein the actuator is axially asymmetric having at least two segments with different elastic properties. 15. The actuator system of any one of claims 9-14 further comprising a rotary selector disposed between the pump and the actuators to enable the pump to selectively supply the actuating fluid to the actuators. 16. An exoskeleton joint comprising: a wearable sleeve; a first member and a second member combined with the wearable sleeve, the second member being pivotably connected to the first member by a hinge; an actuator connected to the first member at its first end and to the second member at its second end, the actuator comprising: an inner member made from an elastic material and defining a compartment for receiving an actuating fluid, the inner member being moveable in a longitudinal direction from a relaxed state to an expanded state by introducing an actuating fluid into the inner member; and 

 an outer member made from an inelastic material and being disposed around the elastic inner member to control expansion of the elastic inner member in a radial direction, the outer member being moveable from a folded relaxed configuration to an unfolded extended configuration as the inner member moves from the relaxed state to the expanded state, wherein the movement of the actuator between the relaxed state and the expanded state cause a movement of at least one of the first member and the second member relative to the other member. 17. The exoskeleton joint of claiml6 wherein the outer member is connected to the elastic inner member discontinuously at one or more contact points. 18. The exoskeleton joint of any one of claims 16-17 wherein the outer member is connected to the elastic inner member discontinuously at a first end and a second end. 19. The exoskeleton joint of any one of claims 16-18 wherein the outer member in a fully extended state forms an uninterrupted outer barrier preventing a radial protrusion of the inner member through the outer member. 20. The exoskeleton joint of any one of claims 16-19 wherein the actuator is axially asymmetric having at least two segments with different elastic properties.
